    Home › Types › Military transports

    Military transports

    Strategic reach, tactical manners, and a tank in the hold.

    Aircraft here2
    Longest75.31 m
    Combined built410
    First flights1968–1991
    What it means

    The category, explained

    A military airlifter has to do two contradictory things: fly intercontinental distances with a strategic payload, then land on a short, unpaved strip and unload without ground equipment. High wings, ramps, and enormous multi-wheel landing gear are the standard answers.

    The C-5 Galaxy and the C-17 Globemaster III between them define Western heavy lift, and both are regularly the first aircraft into a disaster zone. They are the reason a 60-tonne tank or an entire field hospital can cross the world overnight.

    The aircraft

    Every military transport here

    Silhouettes are sized by real wingspan — bigger aircraft draw bigger.

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
